title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Played singleplayer and LAN multiplayer. All custom zombie maps used ran perfectly, as long as you place the unpacked map files in /home/<user>/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/10090/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Activision/CoDWaW/mods. The only issue I could find is the brightness slider not working, couldn't find a fix for that.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Downloaded it from Steam, clicked Play and everything worked perfectly. Tried about half an hour of SP and then went to play MP for couple of hours. Only issue was that I couldn't get console to open, even when it was enabled from options. So no FPS readings but game was butter smooth with 1080p everything maxed.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: I won the single player campaign. 1080P and 2K resolution works good. The game was more stable then playing in windows 2 years ago. I tried to play coop but did not find any games. My only complaint is that Wine has issues if you alt tab out of the game and come back in that the screen is black and you have to restart but Windows has the same issues at times. The game works at 4K but I keep my screen resolution at 2K. Wine/Linux for some reason will not let me game at a higher resolution than my desktop resolution.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: The game runs perfectly, except for the fact that there's a weird lag with the mouse. I've played Windows and Linux and Windows just works. This version doesn't. The worst part is there's forced mouse acceleration. I've tried everything to disable it, from in game to my actual Linux distro. No other game suffers from this acceleration.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

notes: Runs OOTB, however performance is horrendous compared to Windows. On Windows 10 I was getting 144fps consistently if I so chose to raise the cap that high, whereas on Pop!_OS I'm generally hovering between 30-60, with frame pacing issues that cause lots of jitter. Also some minor visual artifacting around water. Playable, but not comfortable by any means.
title: Call of Duty: World at War

cpu: AMD A10-7860K Radeon R7, 12 Compute Cores 4C+8G
notes: I didn't use any fixes the only one that i tried was D9vk in launch options and it made the game crash immediately. Out of the box the game seemed to run fine until you get into a mp game(only tried multi-player ).I had to turn all setting to low just to get to 20-30 fps, It was very laggy no matter the server i went on, it was also very stuttery . IF you are used to low fps then it will be do-able but if you have moderate to high standards than this will be unexeptable/playable. I personally uninstalled it because i couldn't handle the lag and low fps.
title: Call of Duty: World at War
